TEXT [SOPHIA HAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[THE TIME FOR BIOLOGY IS 45 MINUTES, WHICH IS 45 * 60 = 2700 SECONDS. THE TIME FOR CHEMISTRY IS 1 HOUR, WHICH IS 60 MINUTES, WHICH IS 60 * 60 = 3600 SECONDS. TO FIND HOW MANY MORE SECONDS SHE CAN SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN CHEMISTRY COMPARED TO BIOLOGY, WE SUBTRACT THE TIME FOR BIOLOGY FROM THE TIME FOR CHEMISTRY. 3600 - 2700 = 900 SECONDS. SO SHE CAN SPEND 900 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN CHEMISTRY COMPARED TO BIOLOGY.]

[THE ANSWER IS 900 SECONDS. THE WORD TEXT APPEARS EXACTLY ONCE IN THIS RESPONSE. THE WORD PIECE APPEARS EXACTLY TWICE IN THIS RESPONSE. THE WORD ARM APPEARS EXACTLY ONCE IN THIS RESPONSE.
